title: Corrected NPU compute iso-utilization delay separation
hypothesis: The worse delay at larger corrected NPU compute module counts is partly caused by reduced PnR flexibility from higher utilization. Measuring low-nm designs at nm64-like utilization and high-nm designs at nm2-like utilization separates utilization pressure from centralized command/control scaling.

direct_comparison.primary_question: How much of the nm64 delay penalty is explained by utilization rather than num_modules-dependent command distribution and writeback logic?
include: nm2 and nm4 at a compressed floorplan targeting approximately 46% utilization for nm2
nm32 and nm64 at an expanded floorplan targeting approximately 29% utilization for nm64
flat_nomacro and hier_macro mode comparison for each point
actual measured utilization, delay, power, and area rows suitable for fitting delay(nm, utilization)
exclude: claiming final timing stability from a single seed

expected_benefit: prevents perf simulation from overusing optimistic nm2 timing measured at low utilization
prevents monolithic nm64 timing from unfairly penalizing decomposed/tiled architectures
enables a first-order timing model with separate local parallelism and utilization terms
risks: compressed nm2/nm4 floorplans may fail even if the logic is intrinsically fast
expanded nm32/nm64 floorplans may improve routing but not represent a dense tiled macro
single-seed results should guide model structure, not final signoff

required_evaluations.objective: Measure corrected nm2/nm4 NPU compute blocks in a compressed floorplan targeting nm64-like utilization.

expected_result.reason: If nm2/nm4 delay worsens substantially at about 46% utilization, PnR flexibility is a dominant contributor to the nm64 delay penalty.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure corrected nm32/nm64 NPU compute blocks in an expanded floorplan targeting nm2-like utilization for nm64.

expected_result.reason: If nm64 delay improves near low-nm timing at about 29% utilization, PnR flexibility is a dominant contributor; otherwise centralized logic scaling remains significant.
